017package org.apache.juneau.petstore.springboot;
018
019import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
020import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.servlet.*;
021import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.*;
022
023/**
024 * Tiny REST resource that demonstrates Spring {@code @Autowired} injection into a Juneau {@code @Rest} servlet
025 * under a Spring Boot deployment.  Mirrors the legacy {@code HelloWorldResource} demo.
026 *
027 * <p>
028 * The {@link HelloMessageProvider} bean is wired by Spring; the {@code GET /hello/*} endpoint surfaces its message.
029 * Resolution of the {@code HelloMessageProvider} bean works because the parent router extends
030 * {@code BasicSpringRestServletGroup} (i.e. {@code SpringRestServlet}), which lets child resources be Spring beans.
031 *
032 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
033 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauPetstoreOverview">juneau-petstore</a>
034 * </ul>
035 */
036@Rest(
037   path="/hello",
038   title="Hello (Spring injection demo)",
039   description="A trivial REST resource demonstrating Spring @Autowired injection of a HelloMessageProvider bean."
040)
041public class HelloResource extends BasicRestResource {
042
043   private final HelloMessageProvider messageProvider;
044
045   /**
046    * Constructor.
047    *
048    * @param messageProvider The Spring-injected message provider bean.
049    */
050   @Autowired
051   public HelloResource(HelloMessageProvider messageProvider) {
052      this.messageProvider = messageProvider;
053   }
054
055   /**
056    * GET /hello/* — returns the message provided by the injected Spring bean.
057    *
058    * @return The injected message.
059    */
060   @RestGet(path="/*", summary="Responds with the Spring-injected message.")
061   public String sayHello() {
062      return messageProvider.get();
063   }
064}
